post.content:
### Problem
Given two time series data lists,

First List
[(1, 3), (3, 1), (5,3), (6, 4), (10, 1)]
Explanation:
What this means is that at timestamp 1 the value is 3, and then at timestamp 3 the value is 1 and so on.
Note: Missing/Undefined value on any timestamp is extrapolated backwards from the next timestamp. For example, value at timestamp 2 based on the example above would be 1.


Second List
[(2, 3), (6, 3), (9, 2)]


Write a function to aggregate these two time series data into one
Answer
[(1, 6), (2, 4), (3, 4), (5,6), (6, 7), (9, 3), (10,1)]


Solution: Merge sort


### Follow up
which we discuss upon for half of the interview:
What if there are N time series lists are given in above question. I gave divide and conquer approach.

Main Problem:
If both the interval lists are sorted then this become like merging 2 sorted list problem.
1. Pick the smaller one, `newValue = valueSmaller + valueBigger` and move the `smaller` pointer forward;
2. If both the timeStamps are same then `newValue = valueSmaller + ValueBigger` and move `both` the pointers forward.

T: O(M+N)
S: O(M+N)

Followup:
We can maintain `minHeap` for all the heads of the lists, we can also pre-calulate the `aggregate sum` for all these heads. Heap can have elements like - `[timeStamp, listIdx, ElementIdx]`
On every iteration we'll pop out the `minimum` interval and push it in the result as - `[minIntervalTimeStamp, aggregateSum]`.
And now we will modify `aggregateSum` by `subtracting` popped interval value and `adding` the new interval value which will be now added to the heap. (Make sure to remove all the conflicting(starting with the same timestamp) min values from the heap and add the next ones)
Can we use somewhat like a line sweep algorithm here?\nTC: O(n1 + n2 + ....)
for the follow up, doesnt it become equal to merge K sorted lists? also maintain the total sum of elements in pq\n

OP, if you wrote this question from your memory, how come it\'s is same as this one without even a single change in indentation / character.\n\nhttps://leetcode.com/discuss/interview-question/1992821/uber-onsite-coding-question-2022
